page: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a high-density instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Local storage consists of a bundled 3840 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU allocation is shared, with each of the 64 physical cores running a single thread.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong computational capabilities. Its single-core stress-ng performance and passmark CPU physics testing rank in the top tier. Large-block memory bandwidth, both read and write, also reaches top-tier performance, while multi-core stress-ng and passmark CPU scores place in the top 25 percent. For data processing, single-threaded bzip3 decompression is in the top 10 percent, and multi-threaded decompression is in the top 25 percent. Database operations on Passmark and LLM prompt processing for small models also achieve top-tier results.

Because the CPU allocation is shared, this instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale deployments, though it may experience resource contention compared to dedicated-core instances. It is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, data compression tasks, and medium-scale LLM inference.
description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a high-density instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 64 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 processor, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating with a shared CPU allocation, it delivers a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core operations, large-block memory bandwidth, and database execution, alongside strong multi-core processing. This configuration is highly suitable for database management, static web serving, data processing, and medium-scale LLM inference workloads.

tagline: A high-density, shared-core server combining 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs with 3.8 TB of NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
3840 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
